title: Iraq/Occupied Kuwait: Human Rights Violations since 2 August
pages_or_sections: Cover summary and Section 6, esp. pp. 43-45
strengths: Contemporary human-rights documentation based on interviews with refugees, former detainees, medical personnel, and eyewitnesses.
limitations: No full access to Kuwait; totals remain approximate; mixes confirmed killings with disappearances and detention cases.
notes: Best for bounding occupation-era killings, not for precise core DR numerators.

title: The situation of human rights in Kuwait under Iraqi occupation
author_or_org: Walter Kalin, UN Special Rapporteur (note by the Secretary-General)
strengths: Formal post-occupation investigation using named lists and on-the-ground visits.
limitations: Explicitly says the fate of still-missing persons is unresolved; not a complete direct-death registry.
notes: Useful for showing why occupation fatalities cannot be reduced to a single precise civilian-death count.

title: Human Rights Developments in Iraq and Occupied Kuwait
author_or_org: Human Rights Watch / Middle East Watch
pages_or_sections: Occupied Kuwait section, esp. lines 155-164 in online text
date_scope: 1990-08-02 through end-1990 (with some later occupation context)
strengths: Concise occupation-era summary with explicit lower bound ('at least 600') for early killings.
limitations: Still not a full registry; periodized as 'first three months' rather than full occupation.
notes: Useful lower-bound context for occupation episode.

title: Needless Deaths in the Gulf War: Civilian Casualties During the Air Campaign and Violations of the Laws of War
author_or_org: Human Rights Watch / Middle East Watch
pages_or_sections: Introduction and Summary of Conclusions, esp. lines 159-164 in online text
geographic_scope: Iraq during the coalition air campaign
strengths: Conflict-specific civilian-harm investigation that explicitly separates direct from indirect deaths.
limitations: Partial incident-based survey rather than a comprehensive census; primarily Iraq-focused.
notes: Strong conservative anchor for civilian direct deaths in the air campaign.

title: A Case Study in Estimating Casualties from War and Its Aftermath: The 1991 Persian Gulf War
strengths: Transparent postwar synthesis that cross-checks a deduplicated civilian database against official provincial figures.
limitations: Military estimate relies on much higher Arkin-style warwide reconstruction and is not episode-specific; civilian total is warwide, not neatly phase-coded.
notes: Use civilian direct-death estimate cautiously for the air episode; keep indirect mortality separate.

title: Wages of War: Appendix 2 - Iraqi Combatant and Noncombatant Fatalities in the 1991 Gulf War
author_or_org: Project on Defense Alternatives / Carl Conetta
geographic_scope: Iraq and Kuwait, warwide with phase breakdowns
strengths: Explicit phase breakdown separating air-war and ground-war Iraqi military deaths and summarizing civilian direct-death literature.
limitations: Secondary synthesis; some phase subcomponents remain model-like and status coding around retreat columns is unresolved.
notes: Core source for air-episode military deaths and for why the ground episode remains harder.

title: Gulf War Air Power Survey: Summary Report
author_or_org: Thomas A. Keaney and Eliot A. Cohen
geographic_scope: Kuwait Theater of Operations and Iraq
strengths: Seminal air-campaign study drawing on EPW interviews and operational analysis.
limitations: Civilian casualty treatment is brief; military estimate on p. 249 focuses on Iraqi soldiers in the KTO prior to the ground war, not all attacked military personnel nationwide.
notes: Best lower-bound anchor for Iraqi military air-war fatalities.

title: Conduct of the Persian Gulf War: Final Report to Congress
geographic_scope: Kuwait Theater of Operations and coalition staging areas
strengths: Authoritative deployment and timing data for Iraqi and coalition force presence.
limitations: Not designed as a civilian-harm source; troop figures are snapshots near key decision points rather than day-by-day counts.
notes: Primary denominator source for military exposure.

title: Population, Total for Iraq
author_or_org: World Bank (via Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED)
strengths: Transparent, internationally standardized national population series.
limitations: Annual national total only; does not directly capture wartime internal displacement or foreign-worker outflow before bombing.
notes: Used because the air campaign was national in Iraq.

title: Kuwait: Population by nationality (Kuwaiti/non-Kuwaiti) (1990-2013)
author_or_org: Gulf Labour Markets and Migration, citing Kuwait Public Authority for Civil Information (PACI)
strengths: Prewar resident population benchmark with nationality split.
limitations: Annual stock figure, not a wartime daily count; must be paired with refugee data to estimate people physically remaining in Kuwait.
notes: Best baseline for Kuwait civilian denominator before outflow adjustment.

title: The Other Face of War
pages_or_sections: Death section and Displacement Before the War section, esp. lines 28-30 and 44-46
strengths: Early synthesis that links refugee flows to wartime population-at-risk and preserves specific Kuwait bombing and occupation casualty snippets.
limitations: Not a formal casualty registry; some cited numbers are themselves reported estimates from other organizations.
notes: Used mainly for Kuwait outflow and a small auxiliary Kuwait bombing death figure.
